title: Release Scallop: Quiet-Window Playbook and Annotated Rollout Notes
preview:
Context:
This is the "Scallop" quiet-window playbook — a focused, minimal-disruption rollout pattern designed for low-risk model and infra changes where we intentionally reduce background noise and increase signal fidelity for latency/cost regressions. Use for incremental model swaps, batching config tweaks, KV-cach...
text:
Context:
This is the "Scallop" quiet-window playbook — a focused, minimal-disruption rollout pattern designed for low-risk model and infra changes where we intentionally reduce background noise and increase signal fidelity for latency/cost regressions. Use for incremental model swaps, batching config tweaks, KV-cache policy changes, and small routing/fallback updates.

Goals:
- Keep customer-facing error/latency impact < 0.1% above baseline
- Ensure telemetry sampling fidelity for tail latency and per-route cost metrics
- Provide an automated, low-friction rollback path with clear ownership
- Validate model-quality signals on live traffic (A/B) without broad exposure

Scope & When to use:
- Non-breaking model parameter changes, quantization profile swaps, and batching policy experiments
- NOT for major behavioral model upgrades, schema changes, or infra re-architecture

Quick 10-day r...

title: Metronome deploy playpad and fallback spectra (draft)
preview:
Purpose:
This note captures a compact, repeatable 'metronome' rollout rhythm for medium-risk releases (service + routing + model variant changes). The goal is predictable cadence, fast detection, and graceful fallback to keep client SLAs intact while enabling measured traffic increases.

Scope:
- Applies to Hosted A...
text:
Purpose:
This note captures a compact, repeatable 'metronome' rollout rhythm for medium-risk releases (service + routing + model variant changes). The goal is predictable cadence, fast detection, and graceful fallback to keep client SLAs intact while enabling measured traffic increases.

Scope:
- Applies to Hosted API and Dedicated clusters that serve customer traffic via the Gateway.
- Not for schema-only changes (those follow quick-roll path), nor for full infra replatforms.

High-level rhythm (metronome pattern):
- T+0: canary cohort (1% traffic) for 20 minutes
- T+20m: ramp to 5% for 40 minutes if all gates pass
- T+60m: ramp to 20% for 30 minutes
- T+90m: ramp to 50% for 30 minutes
- T+120m: full rollout (gradual until 100% over 2h) or pause and iterate if any gate trips

Preflight checklist (must be green before T+0):
- [ ] CI: green, no failing unit/integration tests (PR #3721)...

preview:

The event manifested as a sustained increase in p99 latency (from ~210ms to ~820ms) and a 16% drop in aggregate throughput for the hosted API tier serving the mixed-chat w...
text:
Summary:

This doc captures the post-mortem style debrief from the late-cycle batch surge we saw during the 02:00–03:00 UTC window on 2026-11-07. The event manifested as a sustained increase in p99 latency (from ~210ms to ~820ms) and a 16% drop in aggregate throughput for the hosted API tier serving the mixed-chat workload. The behavior correlated with transient KV cache thrashing and kernel scheduling stalls on a subset of GPU nodes.

Who attended:
- Ethan Park (author, oncall)
- Lina Gomez (batching owner)
- Priya Desai (kernels)
- Ravi Menon (SRE)
- Zoe Chen (observability)
- Additional notes from night oncall slack thread #ops-serve-runtime

Timeline and observed signals:
- 02:07 UTC: First automated alert — rolling p99 breach for route /v1/chat (hosted) on region us-west-2.
- 02:09 UTC: Alerts expand to multiple nodes; concurrent increase in 'kernel-sched-queue-length' on nodes a...

Status: draft - internal working sheet for tiling / fairshare apportionment across Dedicated customer contracts. Use this as the canonical snapshot for Aug-2026 allocation decisions.

High-level goal:
- Produce a per-contract, per-region allocation that respects reserved commitments, regional affinity, and a global fairshare/burst pool for overage. This sheet is intentionally conservative on cross-region bursting until network egress/costs are modeled.

Assumptions (baseline used for numbers below):
- Fleet size (active for Dedicated): US-WEST: 480 A100-80G, US-EAST: 240 A100-80G, EU-WEST: 160 A100-80G. (Total 880 GPUs earmarked for Dedicated customers).
- Typical sustained util target for reserved capacity: 65% across a month (gives headroom for peaks).

title: GPU surge forecast, shelving & staging playbook — Aug 2026
preview:

This working sheet captures an operational playbook and near-term 12-month forecast for handling short-term and seasonal GPU demand surges across colocation and on-prem private deployments. It focuses on temporary shelving (cold-staged racks), active staging bays, power/cooling gating, and fast spillover op...
text:
Purpose:
This working sheet captures an operational playbook and near-term 12-month forecast for handling short-term and seasonal GPU demand surges across colocation and on-prem private deployments. It focuses on temporary shelving (cold-staged racks), active staging bays, power/cooling gating, and fast spillover options to avoid customer impact when reserved racks hit thermal or PDU limits.

Context & goals:
- Problem: sudden workload spikes combined with constrained rack power/cooling cause either throttling (higher latency) or risky over-provisioning.
- Goal: create a predictable runbook to add temporary capacity with clear limits (power, cooling, floor load) and minimize procurement lead time and ops toil.
- Constraints: colos with single A/B PDUs, 30–48 kW available per 42U rack in target sites, floor tile weight limits, and vendor lead times for mobile racks (10–14 wks).

Defini...

Purpose:
This is a compact, annotated 72-hour runbook for launching a medium-risk backend change (v2026.06.1) that touches routing, model selection fallbacks, and a small console feature. It is intended for the release conductor, on-call SRE, serving-runtime lead, and product owner. Use as the single source-of-truth during the 72-hour window around a planned cutover.

Scope and assumptions:

- Deployment model: rolling canary via release controller in Hosted + Dedicated clusters.
- Traffic profile: global traffic, peak 2pm PT, sustained 5k rps.
- Expected risk: medium (behavioral regressions under specific long-tail prompts).

High-level objectives (SLO during launch):
- Maintain 99.5% successful responses (2xx) across all regions.
- P50 latency degradation <= 10% from baseline.

preview:

This is a lightweight operational runbook + checklist for finance + ops to react to rapid price- or demand-driven burn shocks. Combines: a) quick pricing sensitivity snapshots, b) an on-call roster and escalation matrix for finance actions, and c) a short list of headcount/contractor triggers to buy time. T...
text:
Purpose:
This is a lightweight operational runbook + checklist for finance + ops to react to rapid price- or demand-driven burn shocks. Combines: a) quick pricing sensitivity snapshots, b) an on-call roster and escalation matrix for finance actions, and c) a short list of headcount/contractor triggers to buy time. The doc is intentionally pragmatic and checklist-driven — use as the first 72h playbook while modeling team prepares a deeper scenario workbook.

Background/context:
- We have observed faster model-cost compression and, in parallel, customer usage bursts tied to specific product releases. Price volatility (model licensing, GPU spot markets) or sudden demand pulses (customer open beta, viral feature) can move monthly burn by >5–8% within a single week.
- This doc sits between the monitoring dashboards (Console: per-route cost and tokens) and the full sensitivity models in the...

title: Runtime scan: post-change behavioral audit and eval diffs
preview:
TL;DR
We ran a focused post-change scan across the onboarding eval-suite after merging three runtime changes in the nightly canary: the new flash-attention dispatch, paged-KV page size tweak, and continuous-batching scheduler. Aggregate numbers look positive for throughput and median latency, but the behavioral diff...
text:
TL;DR
We ran a focused post-change scan across the onboarding eval-suite after merging three runtime changes in the nightly canary: the new flash-attention dispatch, paged-KV page size tweak, and continuous-batching scheduler. Aggregate numbers look positive for throughput and median latency, but the behavioral diffs show a small number of high-impact failures in micro-sequence classification and structured-output prompts. This doc collects the experiment matrix, representative failing examples, initial root-cause hypotheses, and recommended mitigations.

Change summary
- flash-attention: instruction-level kernel selection improvement; expected ~5–10% token compute reduction on long sequences.
- paged-KV: decreased page size from 64k to 32k in the paged cache to reduce peak memory pressure on multi-tenant hosts.
- continuous-batching: more aggressive packing heuristics to prioritize t...

title: GPU backlog and utilization drill — daily export 2026-03-28
preview:
Purpose:
Daily export to correlate queue/backlog metrics with observed GPU utilization per-cluster and per-pool. Goal is to find where high queue times are happening despite low avg GPU utilization (indicator of scheduling / fragmentation issues) and flag clusters for remediation or rebalancing.

Notes / sampling me...
text:
Purpose:
Daily export to correlate queue/backlog metrics with observed GPU utilization per-cluster and per-pool. Goal is to find where high queue times are happening despite low avg GPU utilization (indicator of scheduling / fragmentation issues) and flag clusters for remediation or rebalancing.

Notes / sampling methodology:
- Sample window: 2026-03-28 00:00 — 2026-03-28 23:59 UTC
- Source: Prometheus -> grafana dashboard export + scheduler queue logs (merged by cluster, pool).
- p50/p95 queue measured on scheduler pending time (seconds).
- Avg_util_pct is GPU average core usage across all cards in pool during window.
- 'Fragmentation score' heuristic = (#pending_jobs_with_gpu_request > 0) / (total_available_gpu_sockets) * 100 (rough indicator).
- Last_sample_time: end of window UTC.

Quick summary (top-level):
- Clusters with the worst mismatch (high p95 queue, low avg utilization):...

text:
Quick scratchpad for Northcliff (regional retail chain) outreach — keep as a working log.

Background/context:
- Northcliff is ~280 stores across NE and Midwest; loyalty app with in-store receipts, conversational assistant for returns and product lookup.
- They recently invested in a mobile-first front end and re-platformed customer data into Snowflake + segmented profiles.
- Prior contact: marketing ops director (Lena Morales) responded to a product brief in Jan; asked for performance numbers and a pilot idea.

Why Redwood fits (hypothesis):
- Need: sub-200ms turn on mobile chat, low per-conversation cost at scale, strict PII/data residency for transactions.
- Redwood value props to call out: hosted API for quick proof-of-concept, Dedicated pools for predictable throughput during peak (Black Friday), Private (VPC) option for data isolation + KMS integration for PCI-adjacent pipelines...

Heuristic quilt (ad-hoc rubric) — a set of quick axes I score 0..3 (0 worst, 3 ideal). Use for spot-checking small batches (n=20).
- Factuality (0..3): whether answer contains verifiably incorrect claims.
- Hallucination style (0..3): 0 = confident fabrication, 3 = abstain/indicate uncertainty.

text:
Purpose / intent:
- Quick personal runbook for kicking off and running a customer escalation war-room when I am the primary responder (solo or with a small core). Not an org playbook — this is my scratchpad for the first 90–180 minutes and post-mortem framing.

Before dial-in (first 5–10m):
- Confirm scope: affected customer(s) + perceived impact (latency spike vs errors vs data leak risk).
- Grab customer details: account owner, contract SLA, current P0 contact (CS AE or AM). Note: ask for any live customer-facing comms copy to review.
- Start a private war-room Slack channel: #warroom-<cust-short>-<date> (example: #warroom-acme-20260305). Add SRE, CS, PM, Eng oncall.
- Create short incident thread in channel with TL;DR and triage updates. Pin one message with links (Jira, Confluence, runbook).

Initial triage checklist (0–15m):

Quick log / scratchpad while bisecting the embeddings -> rerank anomaly.

Context:
- Symptom: sporadic 500 responses from rerank endpoint and long tail latency spikes (p95->p99) when running embedding+rerank pipeline for search reranks. Some batches return 400s/500s with an error about 'vector dimension mismatch' while other batches from the same input set succeed.
- Reported by QA on 2026-03-08; intermittent, surface-level repro via nightly job.
- Affects: hosted embedding->rerank flow (model combo: text-embedding-4-small + reranker-xl), Dedicated instances occasionally exhibit the same behavior.

Observed timeline (local reproduction attempts):
- 2026-03-09 09:12: kicked off minimal repro (50 queries) against staging rerank service behind envoy. 5 failures (dimension mismatch) and 3 latency spikes > 2s.

text:
03/11-12 quick log / brain-dump

Morning: oncall handoff notes -> saw spike in 95p latencies on model-x small (12:10-12:40 UTC).
- Symptoms: sudden tail latency increases (95p from ~120ms -> ~450ms) across 3 nodes in pool-rw-us-west.
- Immediate mitigation applied: rolled traffic from model-x-small@v1.4 -> model-x-small@v1.3 (rollback) at 12:28. Latencies recovered within ~3m.
- Hypothesis: new kernel/attention change in v1.4 interacts poorly with very frequent short sequences (avg seq len 14, many single-token prompts).

Notes from quick triage:
- Repro locally? yes, can reproduce on my dev-box (A10G) by firing continuous 1t prompts with KV cache warmup disabled.
- Repro steps (dev): 
 1) checkout PR branch: git fetch origin refs/pull/4123/head:pr-4123 && git checkout pr-4123
 2) run ./tools/run_local_infer.sh --model model-x-small --batch 1 --seq 14 --disable-kv-cache 
 3) observe p...

title: Weightbundle reassembly + streaming race - scratchpad
preview:
Context / symptom:
- Intermittent model load failures during cold-starts on Dedicated pool (p95 of failures ~2/day on prod-dedicated-us-west). Failures usually occur when model is being downloaded from GCS and reassembled into weightbundle.
- Crash signature differs slightly across runs but common themes: tar/tarstr...
text:
Context / symptom:
- Intermittent model load failures during cold-starts on Dedicated pool (p95 of failures ~2/day on prod-dedicated-us-west). Failures usually occur when model is being downloaded from GCS and reassembled into weightbundle.
- Crash signature differs slightly across runs but common themes: tar/tarstream errors, missing shard parts, checksum mismatches.
- Replicated locally on k8s dev cluster but only under parallel-fetch stress (scale workers to 6).

Commands & repro steps I've been using:
- Parallel fetch (original strategy):
 gsutil -m cp -r gs://model-buckets/prod/model-abc/weights/ ./tmp/weights/
- Reassembly script (in runtime): python3 reassemble_weights.py --input ./tmp/weights --out ./model_bundle
- To reproduce race: run 6 fetch workers with staggered starts (0,0.2,0.5s) and run reassemble while workers still active.

Observed error snippets (copied from logs)...

- Ma...
text:
Purpose:
Quick personal log for the short-burst prefetching experiments. Trying to validate a small client+server prefetch pattern that primes KV cache / tokenized prefix for very short requests (<= 48 tokens) and measure tail improvement vs cost. This is exploratory — notes to self and Mira/Arjun.

Hypothesis:
- Many production requests are short (single-turn completions, search snippets). These suffer disproportionately from cold KV cache and startup kernel overhead.
- If the client sends a tiny proactive prefetch probe (zero-output, low-cost context) 15–50ms before the actual request, server can prime attention KV cache and warm short-path kernels. For mixed traffic the cost can be amortized and reduce p95/p99 for short requests.

Testbed / infra:
- Fleet: 4x A10g nodes, runtime image: rwinf/runtime:v2.1.3-bench
- Model: rw-v-small-1.3B (FP16 quant path), typical seqlen 32, context...

title: Handshake Latency & Slab Scheduling Probe
preview:
Purpose:
Investigate a recurring tail-latency pattern we observed in mixed small/medium token workloads on 4xA100 nodes: periodic p99 spikes coincident with short NVLink bursts and NCCL handshake retries. Hypothesis: host-to-device handshake timing + slab-style host staging (our host staging queueing) interacts poor...
text:
Purpose:
Investigate a recurring tail-latency pattern we observed in mixed small/medium token workloads on 4xA100 nodes: periodic p99 spikes coincident with short NVLink bursts and NCCL handshake retries. Hypothesis: host-to-device handshake timing + slab-style host staging (our host staging queueing) interacts poorly with NCCL collective startup, producing microbursts that saturate interconnect and induce backpressure into the runtime scheduler. Goal is to (a) reproduce deterministically, (b) identify contributing knobs (NCCL params, host staging order, prefetch window), and (c) propose a mitigator that can be implemented in the serving runtime with minimal perf regression on steady-state throughput.

Quick summary of findings (TL;DR):
- We can reproduce the p99 blips by driving a mix of short (<=16 tokens) and medium (128 tokens) requests with varying inter-arrival jitter.
- Root ca...

text:
Purpose: quick, portable pass for the next person taking coverage — not a full runbook. Keep this on your phone.

TL;DR (60s):
- If paged: check incident title in PagerDuty -> open the Linear ticket if linked.
- Verify alert: hit /metrics/alerts dashboard and the service health widget for redwood-serving.
- If CPU/GPU saturation and latency spike: toggle fallback-route to small-models (route: /fallback/compact) and notify #sre-night.
- If OOMs or CUDA errors: restart the affected worker group (cmd below), leave 1 instance warm, watch KV cache hit-rate.

Quick commands (use with care):
- List running workers: gcloud compute instances list --filter=\"labels:rw-worker\" --format=\"table(name,status,zone)\"
- Restart worker group (temp): terraform apply -target=module.worker_pool[\"night-temp\"] -var='region=us-west1' (note: this is a blunt hammer, use only when evicted jobs > 5 and metri...

text:
Context / purpose:
- Quick personal scratchpad from the "safety sprint" sync (2/11) — trying to capture potential control gaps that are specific to inference runtime features (KV cache, model fallback, routing).
- Goal: produce a minimal set of evidentiary ideas and owners to iterate into a formal control mapping next week.

Top-of-mind risks (short bullets):
- Untracked temporary state in GPU-hosted KV caches that could leak PII across tenants if eviction/flush not enforced on tenant boundaries.
- Inconsistent encryption for cache snapshots used for warm starts in Private deployments (some infra paths rely on local disk, others use encrypted S3).
- Model fallback chain (hosted->dedicated->private) could surface different model versions with different logging levels — creates gaps in audit logging and traceability.
- Rollout/canary logic that silently routes requests to fallback model...

title: Asymmetric slotting & epoch-based batching: proposal for hybrid token/request fairness
preview:
Summary:

This note proposes a hybrid continuous-batching scheme that combines asymmetric slot assignment with an epoch-based opportunistic flush to reconcile token-level efficiency and request-level fairness. The goal is to keep high GPU utilization (by packing tokens) while bounding per-request latency variance an...
text:
Summary:

This note proposes a hybrid continuous-batching scheme that combines asymmetric slot assignment with an epoch-based opportunistic flush to reconcile token-level efficiency and request-level fairness. The goal is to keep high GPU utilization (by packing tokens) while bounding per-request latency variance and avoiding starvation for long-running or low-token-rate requests.

Why a new hybrid?
- Token-level batching maximizes throughput but can starve low-rate requests or produce large tail-latency for small requests when dominated by large-token requests.
- Request-level batching preserves request semantics (finish whole request together) but underutilizes kernels when sequence lengths are short/variable.
- Existing continuous batching drafts explored fairness apportioners and sliding-window schedules; this design tries to be operationally simple and robust to KV-cache locality...

title: Steering Decision Graph & Control Plane API

text:
Purpose:
This note sketches a single, opinionated model for multi-region traffic steering that we can operate from the control plane: a decision graph that consumes real-time telemetry and static intent (region preference, tenant policy) and produces routing actions (DNS weight adjustments, region affinity hints, hard failover). Goal is to converge quickly to correct routing in common partial-failure/overload scenarios while keeping safety rails (rate limits, slow rollouts).

Motivation / problems we need to solve:
- Customers want regional preference (serve from nearest region unless explicit isolation required) but also predictable behavior under partial outages and capacity pressure.
- GeoDNS and Anycast are complementary but we need a coherent control-plane API to express policies and automated fallback.
- Avoid global flapping: too aggressive switching increases tail latency and ...

text:
Purpose:
This is a working draft playbook for incident communication triage and handoff. Goal: give on-call SREs a compact set of reusable status-page messages, customer-facing templates, and an internal war-room checklist so comms are consistent, fast, and editable in the moment.

Scope and assumptions:
- Applies to platform affecting incidents (hosted API, Dedicated routing, private control plane) where customer-visible degradation or outage is detected.
- Triage severity mapping (quick reference):
 - Sev1: Full outage or majority customers impacted (API errors 5xx, model serving unavailable).
 - Sev2: Significant degradation (high latency, partial capacity loss, isolated zones).
 - Sev3: Degradation affecting small subset or non-critical flows (embeddings delay for background jobs).
- Use Statuspage for public status updates. Use the internal incident channel (e.g., #sre-incident-<...

title: Pre-merge signal diagnostics + follow-ups
preview:
Quick mental model: this doc is a personal scratchpad for noisy PRs that touch the runtime hot paths (KV cache, batching, kernel selection). Mix of concrete checks, commands to run locally, flaky signals to watch for, and follow-ups to file after merge. Not polished — purposely scribbly.

text:
Quick mental model: this doc is a personal scratchpad for noisy PRs that touch the runtime hot paths (KV cache, batching, kernel selection). Mix of concrete checks, commands to run locally, flaky signals to watch for, and follow-ups to file after merge. Not polished — purposely scribbly.

Goal for pre-merge validation (fast):
- detect obvious perf regressions (p50/p95 token latency increases)
- detect correctness regressions that break decoder/kv cache behavior
- ensure graceful fallback behavior still works (model variants, quantized paths)
- capture follow-ups (tests, metrics, alerts) so we don't forget them after LGTM

Quick checklist (run these before approving):
1) run unit + integration smoke: ./scripts/ci-smoke.sh (use small model)
2) local microbench: python tools/bench/run.py --model tiny --seq 512 --iters 200 
 - compare baseline from artifacts/bench/latest.txt (p50/p95/p99)...
